structured_content.text:
View
                        email in browser The Grenell Snub, Wall Street Future-Proofing, NFL Growing Pains Welcome back to The Daily Courant, your afternoon assortment of Puck’s best new reporting. First up today, Eriq Gardner spotlights the latest twist in the long-running legal feud between Warner Bros. TV and the fiercely protective Michael Crichton estate over Max’s new medical drama, The Pitt , which emerged just as talks to reboot ER —the ’90s NBC juggernaut that Crichton created—fell apart. It may not be a copyright case, per se, but it could have real implications for how “derivative works” are assessed. Plus, below the fold: Tara Palmeri details the blowback inside Mar-a-Lago as Donald Trump begins stocking his administration. Marion Maneker turns a klieg light on the Cecily Brown phenomenon. On The Town , Matt Belloni is joined by director R.J. Cutler to candidly discuss Martha Stewart ’s disapproval of his new Netflix
documentary. On The Varsity , John Ourand and NBC Sports’ Mike Florio unpack the biggest issues facing the NFL today. And on The Powers That Be , Bill Cohan joins Peter Hamby to examine how Wall Street is pricing in the second Trump era—the tariffs, the tax cuts, and a fresh wave of corporate bullying. The Reskinning of an ‘ER’ Reboot ERIQ GARDNER Warner Bros. TV has fired back at Sherri Crichton’s lawsuit over Max’s new medical drama, ‘The Pitt,’ which she claims is little more than an ‘ER’ knockoff. Max claims that Sherri is trying to monopolize the medical genre. But it’s not that simple. The Grenell Snub, Rubio’s Rise & The MAGA Divide TARA PALMERI Tensions arise in Mar-a-Lago as Trump bypasses longtime confidant Ric Grenell for Marco Rubio as Secretary of State, sparking angst among the MAGA faithful and perpetuating questions about Trump’s loyalty. A Cecily Brown Christmas MARION MANEKER A major retrospective at the Dallas Museum of Art, plus a show at the Paula Cooper Gallery, make the case for the painter as one of the most compelling, intellectually and politically engaged artists at work today. Martha Stewart & When a Celeb Disavows Their Doc—With the Director! MATTHEW BELLONI Matt is joined by R.J. Cutler, director of the Netflix documentary ‘Martha’ and the upcoming film ‘Elton John: Never Too Late,’ to talk about Martha Stewart’s disapproval of the film, directors maintaining creative control over their projects, and the inherent and necessary struggle of collaborating with a major celebrity on a documentary. NFL Growing Pains JOHN OURAND John is joined by NBC Sports’ Mike Florio, creator of ProFootballTalk, to contemplate the biggest dramas facing the NFL today: the debate surrounding whether (and how) to add an additional game to the schedule, the push to expand its international footprint, the league’s surprising difficulty selling NFL media, and much more. Wall Street’s Trump 2.0 Trade PETER HAMBY & WILLIAM D. COHAN Bill Cohan joins Peter to examine how Wall Street is pricing the second Trump era—the tariffs, the tax cuts, and all the corporate bullying. Bill runs down the list of familiar faces jockeying for Cabinet positions (Gary, Schwarzman, Paulson…), plus some surprising names that are suddenly in contention. Then they discuss how investors are playing the market rally, and whether crypto will get a Trump bump. Need help? Review our FAQs
                                    page or contact
                                    us for assistance. For brand partnerships, email ads@puck.news . You received this email because you signed up to receive emails from Puck, or as part of your Puck account associated with puck@khamel.com. To stop receiving this newsletter and/or manage all your email preferences, click here . Puck is published by Heat Media LLC. 227 W 17th St New York, NY 10011.
